title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Browser and Node Cli… · Type-System Trace: Json Boundary Validation
summary: A browser data panel negotiates JSON and CSV, receives compressed content, and must validate unknown fields before rendering an aria-live update. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts json boundary validation increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch representation negotiation and validation boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Serialize intentional JSON and validate unknown decoded values before admitting them to trusted domain types. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
